step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with agent1’s assessment. The key features mentioned—vascular plaques on cranial nerves, presence from birth without morphological changes—are highly indicative of Sturge-Weber syndrome (SWS). These findings match typical presentations of SWS, including involvement of the trigeminal nerve and consistent stability throughout life. No other option fits these criteria as precisely.

Answer: A Agreeing fully based on provided evidence and characteristics matching those of SWS. Your analysis reinforces my initial agreement with Option A, Sturge-Weber syndrome.
